transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I have a sore throat.
When did your sore throat start?
2 days ago.
Do you have any fever, chills, or swollen glands?
I have all of those. Fever, chills, and swollen glands.
Any difficulty breathing or swallowing?
No.
Any cough, runny nose, or nasal congestion?
No.
Have you noticed any white patches or pus on your tonsils or throat?
Yes. I have.
Have you had any prior episodes of sore throat like this, or any history of rheumatic fever or heart problems?
No.
Any exposure to someone with strep throat or recent close contacts with similar symptoms?
Yes. My son had stripped through it last week.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Penicillin.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Female.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you have symptoms consistent with strep throat, which is a bacterial infection that can cause sore throat, fever, and swollen glands. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or do tests, but based on what you told me, medication treatment may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review this note and, if appropriate, may prescribe an antibiotic that’s safe for you given your penicillin allergy. In the meantime, you can take acetaminophen (Tylenol) or ibuprofen (Advil) to help with pain and fever if you can take them safely. If you develop trouble breathing or swallowing, or if your symptoms get worse or don’t improve in the next couple of days, please seek in-person care right away at an urgent care or emergency room. A doctor will review your case soon and decide on the best treatment.
